manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: play, india, drop, shadow, ham, theater, war, diamond, poison.
Your opponent's team words are: square, amazon, america, himalayas, note, shot, calf, lemon.
Distractor words are: agent, sound, tablet, port, duck, london, spider, trunk.
The kill words are: .

message.content:
CLUE: Curtain
TARGETS: play, theater, drop, shadow
message.reasoning:
We need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words, and list the target words. Must not be any of the listed words or contain parts of them. Provide clue and targets. The list of our words: play, india, drop, shadow, ham, theater, war, diamond, poison.

We want a clue that relates to multiple of them. Avoid opponent and distractor words. Also avoid kill words; but kill words list empty. Good.

Find a clue that connects e.g., "stage" relates to theater, play, maybe shadow? Not sure. "Blood" relates to war, poison, ham? maybe not. "Assassin" relates to poison, war, drop? hmm.

Better clue: "Cinderella" relates to play (as a story), theater, maybe drop (as in drop a ball?), but not great.

Maybe clue "Guns" relates to war, poison? Not.

How about "Black" relates to shadow, diamond (black diamond), ham? no. "Royal" relates to diamond (royal diamond), India (Royal India), theater (royal theater), war (royal war). Not great.

Maybe "Spice" relates to India, ham (spiced ham), poison (spice can be poison), theater? maybe not.

Maybe "Curtain" relates to theater, play, shadow (curtain casts shadow), drop (curtain drop). That is good. Curtain drop is a term in theater when the performance ends. It also relates to play, theater. Shadow: curtains cast shadows. So clue "Curtain". However "curtain" contains "curt" part of "curt"? Not relevant. It's not in any list. Good.

But does "curtain" relate to "drop"? The phrase "curtain drop". Could be considered. Might be okay.

List targets: play, theater, drop, shadow maybe? Might be okay.

We need to provide list. Let's include play, theater, drop, shadow. That's 4.

Check that "curtain" is not one of listed words. Not.

Make sure not contain parts of them: "curt" no.

Thus output:

CLUE: Curtain
TARGETS: play, theater, drop, shadow

Make sure format exactly.

Provide a comma-separated list of up to 4 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'curtain'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

war, play, amazon, india, tablet, drop, sound, agent, london, duck, lemon, himalayas, poison, shot, shadow, calf, port, america, ham, square, spider, trunk, diamond, note, theater
